headline: South Florida's real estate & tech boom keeps rolling — billionaires flooding in, mega-projects coming online

stories.title: Miami Ranked #5 for Net Inbound Migration in US
stories.summary: Miami's climbing the rankings as a destination for relocation, driven by its strong job market and appeal to wealthy residents. The city's economic momentum is legit fr.
stories.takeaway: People are literally moving to Miami in droves—unemployment sits at just 2.4%, making this the place to be if you're hunting for work.

stories.title: FAA Approves Permits for 47-Story Mixed-Use Tower in Fort Lauderdale
stories.summary: 11 Andrews, a major downtown development, just got the green light to rise 496 feet. This is another big win for FTL's skyline transformation.
stories.takeaway: Fort Lauderdale's downtown is lowkey becoming a real estate powerhouse—mega-towers keep getting approved as the city doubles down on development.

stories.title: Fort Lauderdale's Downtown Economic Impact Surges 20% Since 2022
stories.summary: Tech, finance, and creative industries are driving serious growth in downtown FTL—total economic impact jumped 44% since 2019. The 'Wall Street of the South' is no joke.
stories.takeaway: Fort Lauderdale's becoming a legit rival to Miami with major tech headquarters like Napster Corp moving in, plus nearly 117k tech workers already based there.

stories.title: Miami Beach Hotels Hit Highest Rates Globally for New Year, Occupancy at 93%
stories.summary: Miami Beach crushing it in hospitality—avg daily room rates hit $353 in January (up 8% YoY) with peak occupancy reaching 93%. The city's basically sold out.
stories.takeaway: Tourists are spending big on South Florida stays, which means serious revenue for hotels & the broader economy. This momentum keeps building.

watchNext.title: FAA Cuts Flights by 10% at Miami & Fort Lauderdale Airports
watchNext.why: Flight reductions due to staffing pressures will impact travel & commerce in South Florida—worth monitoring how this affects business travel.

watchNext.title: Fort Lauderdale Convention Center Expansion Opening 2026
watchNext.why: The massive expansion adds 525k sq ft of space & a new waterfront ballroom—this could seriously boost event business & tourism revenue.
